Frieren: Beyond Journey's End Season 2 English Dub Episode 1 Cast Frieren voiced by Mallorie Rodak Fern voiced by Jill Harris Stark voiced by Jordan Dash Cruz Eisen voiced by Christopher Guerrero Himmel voiced by Clifford Chapin Heiter voiced by Jason Douglas Dach voiced by Bradley Gareth Ehre voiced by Trina Nishimura Hero of the South voiced by Brian Mathis Sword Demon voiced by Jennifer Green Scharf voiced by Alejandro Saab Wirbel voiced by Ricco Fajardo Coachman voiced by Doug Jackson Frieren: Beyond Journey's End Season 2 English Dub Episode 1 Crew Voice Director: Jad Saxton Producer: Susie Nixon Adaptation: Macy Anne Johnson Mixer: Matt Grounds Engineer: Seth Aulds RELATED: Crunchyroll Winter 2026 Anime Dubs Include JUJUTSU KAISEN Season 3, Frieren: Beyond Journey's End Season 2 and More Key Art RELATED: Why I’m So Excited for Frieren Fridays to Be Back! Adapting the award-winning manga by Kanehito Yamada and Tsukasa Abe, Keiichiro Saito ( BOCCHI THE ROCK! ) directed the first season of Frieren: Beyond Journey's End at studio MADHOUSE, with series composition by Tomohiro Suzuki ( One-Punch Man ), character designs by Reiko Nagasawa ( takt op.Destiny ) and music by Evan Call ( Violet Evergarden ). Tomoya Kitagawa takes over as director for Season 2. Crunchyroll streams the anime in a range of subs and dubs, describing the story like so: After the party of heroes defeated the Demon King, they restored peace to the land and returned to lives of solitude. Generations pass, and the elven mage Frieren comes face to face with humanity’s mortality. She takes on a new apprentice and promises to fulfill old friends’ dying wishes. Can an elven mind make peace with the nature of life and death? Frieren embarks on her quest to find out.

item.title: Hell Teacher: Jigoku Sensei Nube Cour 2 Anime Adds Tomokazu Sugita to Cast
item.author: Joseph Luster
item.description: Sugita joins the series to voice Daidarabocchi in episode 18
item.content:encoded: Another new cast member joins the ranks of the Hell Teacher: Jigoku Sensei Nube anime during its ongoing second cour. The latest addition is Tomokazu Sugita (Gintoki Sakata in Gintama ), who will voice Daidarabocchi starting in episode 18. Serialized in Weekly Shonen Jump magazine from 1993 to 1999, the Hell Teacher: Jigoku Sensei Nube manga was previously adapted into a 49-episode television anime, plus several films and OVAs by Toei Animation, as well as a 10-episode live-action drama and a PlayStation video game. Yasuyuki Oishi directs the new anime at Studio KAI, with series composition by Yoshiki Okusa, character designs by Yuu Yoshiyama and music by Evan Call ( Frieren: Beyond Journey's End ). REMOW describes the story of Hell Teacher: Jigoku Sensei Nube as such: The town of Domori has been plagued by several inexplicable phenomena. To protect the town's children, a new homeroom teacher named Nube arrives. Though normally gentle and somewhat outgoing, Nube has a secret side: he is the only psychic teacher in Japan. Rumor has it that his left hand is possessed by a demon. The messenger of Hell's justice is here to take on the school's seven mysteries, ghosts, and evil spirits attacking his students. This occult action story starts now! Source: Official X (formerly Twitter)

item.title: Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream Video Highlights New Features, Release Date
item.author: Joseph Luster
item.description: First entry in the series in 10 years hits Switch platforms on April 16
item.content:encoded: Custom Mii characters are finally back in the spotlight thanks to Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream . Nintendo held a Direct presentation earlier today to shed more light on the first entry in the series in over a decade, showing off new features along with April 16, 2026 launch plans for Nintendo Switch. The new Tomodachi Life will also be playable on Switch 2 when it launches. You can see more of what it has to offer, from detailed customization options to the way your characters develop as their lives go on in real time, in the 22-minute video below. Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream Direct Here’s more on the new entry from Nintendo: Mii Characters With Character – In Tomodachi Life: Living the Dream, players can fully customize and envision Mii characters as family, friends and more. Your experience can differ based on the Miis you create as residents, so feel free to experiment and grow your island population! Create Many Miis – There are two ways to create Mii characters: Choose “Get Help” to answer questions about their facial features, or “From Scratch” to select from various face types, hairstyles, eyes and more. There are many parts to choose from, some of which are new to this game, and you can create even more imaginative looking Miis with robust face paint options, too. After that, you can customize their name, height, body type, gender, voice and aspects of their personality, including how quirky they are, their energy levels and more. Help Residents Connect – Once created, Miis will need some help getting on their feet. One way to do this is to bring various residents together. Drop Miis near each other and different situations might play out: They may discuss their favorite foods or bond over their love of monster trucks. Once acquainted, the Miis will be able to interact on their own. You can also have up to eight residents live together as roommates, and they’ll react differently when sharing a home. With multiple Miis under one roof, some unexpected dramas may unfold! Visit Island Shops and Facilities – Help Miis discover their favorite eats and culinary curiosities at Fresh Kingdom food mart ; pick up a range of everyday attire and out-there outfits at Where & Wear clothing ; revamp rooms and living spaces at T&C Reno home supply (there are even rooms for Miis who dream of sleeping in a supermarket or a library!); shop for reasonably priced items that change depending on the time of day at the Marketplace ; gather island decorations at Quik Build amenities ; tune into the MNN news station to keep up with the Miis and everyday happenings on the island; and stage a fun photo op for Miis at Foto-Tomo photography and more. Palette House Workshop – Create a wide range of elements at this artsy workshop to further enliven island life! Design pets for Mii characters, draft up custom drinks, sketch a resident’s favorite TV show, decorate a special item of clothing, draw the exterior of a house and ground tiles, and go wild by creating original items for your island, too. Make Your Island Yours – Change up your island’s aesthetic with landscape items, such as trees, plants, benches, vending machines, bouncy playground rides and more. Move shops and houses around, expand areas of land, and more based on suggestions from residents or personal preference. More Ways to Customize – And add some flourishes to Mii characters by gifting them little quirks that can give them even more individuality, like poses they may strike, how they enjoy their food or having them toss and turn while they sleep, for example. You can also gift them favorite phrases to spice up their sentences. Try finding little quirks that fit each Mii! Source: Press release

item.title: I Got Married to the Girl I Hate Most in Class Audiobook and More Coming from Seven Seas
item.author: Joseph Luster
item.description: Publisher announces six more manga and three audioboks
item.content:encoded: Manga and audiobooks are the flavor of the week for Seven Seas Entertainment’s latest licensing roundups. Six manga titles were announced on Wednesday alongside three audiobooks, lining February dates up for the audio debuts of I Abandoned My Engagement Because My Sister is a Tragic Heroine, but Somehow I Became Entangled with a Righteous Prince , Easygoing Territory Defense by the Optimistic Lord: Production Magic Turns a Nameless Village into the Strongest Fortified City and I Got Married to the Girl I Hate Most in Class . Manga release windows range from September to December 2026. Check out this week’s full lineup of announcements below. I Abandoned My Engagement Because My Sister is a Tragic Heroine, but Somehow I Became Entangled with a Righteous Prince Audiobook - Siren Imprint by Fuyutsuki Koki and Haduki Futaba, with narration by Mirai February 19, 2026 Synopsis: Leia's life is turned upside down after her younger sister claims she’s a cruel villain. Leia’s fiancé breaks off their engagement, leaving her no choice but to throw herself into her saintly duties. But when confronted by the righteous Crown Prince Eric, she finds herself under his scrutiny. He’s determined to suss out her true nature, but why does it involve living in the palace with him?! Easygoing Territory Defense by the Optimistic Lord: Production Magic Turns a Nameless Village into the Strongest Fortified City Audiobook - Siren Imprint by Sou Akaike and Kururi, with narration by Eric O'Keeffe February 26, 2026 Synopsis: Van, fourth son of a marquis, is just a toddler when he realizes he's been reincarnated. Thanks to his literal lifetime of knowledge, he's raised as a child prodigy—until his production magic manifests, and it's the last thing his snooty mage family wants to see! His disappointed father banishes him to a podunk town on the verge of collapse, yet Van can only see the place's potential. Can our hero's bastion of battlements build a better life than battle magic ever could?! I Got Married to the Girl I Hate Most in Class Audiobook - Siren Imprint by Amano Seiju, Nanami Narumi, and Mosskonbu, with narration by Mark Sanderlin February 12, 2026 Synopsis: Archenemies Hojou Saito and Sakuramori Akane have a notorious reputation as bitter rivals at their high school. Without fail, the two can be found bickering and arguing every day, making school their own personal battleground. But their relationship takes a drastic turn when Saito’s domineering grandfather delivers a shocking ultimatum: marry his rival or lose the family business to the dog! Saito reluctantly accepts his fate, and to his complete surprise, Akane agrees without a fight. And just like that, they’re a married couple! Now, these rivals-turned-newlyweds must navigate living under the same roof and sharing meals (and a bed!) all without tearing each other apart. Can they manage to live together in peace and keep their embarrassing circumstances a secret from their classmates? I Don't Know, Yoshida-san Mature-rated Manga - Ghost Ship Imprint by Osamu Takahashi November 2026 Synopsis: Kutsumo is a perfectly average university student with no redeeming qualities whatsoever. One day, while heading home after a perfectly normal day of lectures, he spots beautiful Yoshida. Over a loud fuss the other boys make, she quietly beckons to him. Once the two are alone, Yoshida’s aloof demeanor changes into one of passion. In truth, the two are in a secret relationship. But with her cold personality, he’s never sure just where he stands. Can Kutsumo turn Yoshida’s private desire into a blaze that cannot be contained? An Ordinary Guy’s Crazy Modern Dungeon Survival! Manga by Shibainubutai, Mochiron san, and Masahiro Suwa December 2026 Synopsis: The “Great Hole of Babel” holds monsters beyond human understanding—making it the ultimate spot for explorers! Tadahito Miyama is one of them, but he’s got one unique advantage. Blessed with an ability that can produce key hints and strategies, Miyami holds the power to conquer any beast he comes across. But after living a life where everyone has always looked down on him, Miyama has little ambition and lacks the drive to pursue any notions of greatness. Will that change when he learns his power can surpass even the strongest of heroes? Or is he destined to live in mediocrity? Love Tattoo Boys' Love Manga by Akairo Mash December 2026 Synopsis: Naoki couldn't help finding Aisuke captivating. Charming, good-looking, and a talented tattoo artist, he is everything that salaryman Naoki isn’t. Perhaps it was due to his recent circumstances—a growing boredom with his humdrum life and the recent breakup with his fiancé—but Naoki is completely drawn to Aisuke the moment he passes by the tattoo shop. But there’s more to the beautifully inked-up artist who holds a past flame that he still wears on his chest. Can the two meet each other where they are and find comfort in one another? 10 Things I Want to Do Before I Turn 40 - Part 2 Mature-rated Boys' Love Manga by MAMITA September 2026 Synopsis: Suzume Tōjō, freshly forty, has done the impossible—found love again after a decade-long dry spell! His new boyfriend is none other than his handsome junior at work, the ten years younger Keishi Tanaka. Now the real challenge begins: keeping their professional and private lives from crashing into each other! Their relationship rules are simple: Maintain the same distance at the office. No pet names on the clock! Don’t spend the whole day staring at Keishi. Good behavior earns a special reward… Can Suzume survive the temptation of a gorgeous younger partner at the office? The smash-hit, high-heat romance continues! Mii-chan and Yamada-san Manga by NENE AZUKI December 2026 Synopsis: In the heart of Shinjuku, there are a variety of host and hostess nightclubs open to customers seeking a night of indulgence with a gorgeous companion. Yamada is one of those companions. She balances university and work, but finds herself more interested in becoming a model hostess than a star pupil. The pay is too tempting to resist, which is why so many girls come and go in the industry. But in 2012, she meets someone completely different. Mii-chan is a petite young woman who seems to be rather useless at most things—barely able to read or write and, unfortunately, clumsy—earning her ridicule from staff and customers alike. However, her bright energy and endless motivation proves hard to ignore, and Yamada finds herself drawn in by Mii-chan's charm. A beautiful and tragic story about an unlikely friendship and the unfortunate end that separated them forever. Dinosaur Sanctuary: Let's Go to Dinoland Manga Picture Book by Itaru Kinoshita November 2026 Synopsis: Join Yukari and her little brother Souma on a trip to Enoshima Dinoland, a zoo for dinosaurs! There, you'll meet a gentle old Tyrannosaurus, a short-tempered Spinosaurus, and many more, each with their own quirks and personalities. But watch out—as you roam around Dinoland with Yukari and Souma, you might just find yourselves wandering back in time to dinosaur days… This picture book set in the world of the beloved manga Dinosaur Sanctuary is packed with dino facts and detailed artwork based on paleontological research. It's sure to delight dinosaur lovers of all generations! Source: Press releases

item.title: 10 Anime With the Most Shocking Plot Twists
item.author: Callie Cadorniga
item.description: How could anyone see these coming?
item.content:encoded: Nothing spices up an anime like a good plot twist, whether it's in a mystery series, shonen, romance, psychological thriller, or even a magical girl series. It can be exciting when your favorite anime pulls the rug out from under you and changes things up in a shocking way. Some of our favorite twists in anime come in all sorts of forms. Some smack you right on the face, while others are slow burns that lead to some jaw-dropping payoff. We present, in no particular order, some of the most shocking and memorable anime twists that have left us gobsmacked. NOTE: As you might expect, this list will feature massive spoilers for several anime, so continue at your own discretion. [OSHI NO KO] The twist: Ai's fate and her children's identities When you’re reincarnated in an anime, you usually get sent to a high fantasy world where your abilities are both absurdly powerful and take on an ironic form, such as a slime or a spider. But not in the high-stakes entertainment world of [OSHI NO KO] . The extended first episode centers on Dr. Gorou Amamiya as he becomes the private physician of popular idol Ai Hoshino, who is pregnant with twins. Ironically, he became a fan of Ai after spending time with Sarina Tendouji, a former patient of his who died from a terminal illness. After being killed by one of Ai’s stalkers, Amamiya is reincarnated as one of Ai’s children, a son named Aquamarine. Unbeknownst to him, his twin sister is the reincarnation of Sarina. The two of them attain a newfound appreciation for their mother as Ai becomes more popular by the year. However, her star burns out in a tragic and untimely manner. The stalker who previously killed Amamiya tracks down Ai in their new home and fatally stabs her before committing suicide. In her final moments, Ai professes her love for her children before dying in front of them. In the midst of their mourning, Aqua realizes that the stalker must have received Ai’s home address from their biological father. He then vows to infiltrate the entertainment industry to track down and kill their father. Years later, he and Aqua enter high school as Aqua sets his sights on revenge. Puella Magi Madoka Magica The twist: Homura's true ability As a popular and classic deconstruction of the magical girl franchise, Puella Magi Madoka Magica is no stranger to changing up the formula. By the series’ midpoint, we already discover that a contract with Kyubey to become a magical girl comes at the cost of your soul. What’s more, any magical girl is in danger of becoming a Witch, much like the ones that the current girls have been eliminating in the first place. But just when you think that this dark, classic magical girl anime is done throwing you for a loop, it has one more trick up its sleeve. When we first meet Homura Akemi, she’s introduced as a stoic and charismatic girl. She’s mysterious, skilled at fighting and quite cold when it comes to her interactions with Madoka. However, we later learn that she’s closer to Madoka than just about anyone. In reality, Homura uses her time control powers to reset the same month over and over again. She was originally a timid and weak-willed girl who was saved from a Witch by Madoka in another timeline. When Madoka perishes in the fight against an all-powerful Witch, Homura asks Kyubey to make her into a magical girl and return to her first meeting with Madoka to save her. Unfortunately, her efforts are often in vain as Madoka inevitably falls no matter how much Homura tries to change past events. Despite this, she’s determined to repeat history as often as she needs to in order to save Madoka. My Hero Academia The twist: Dabi's origins Admittedly, plenty of fans saw this one coming from a mile away. After all, the nature of Quirks in the world of My Hero Academia makes it easy for folks to trace their origins. But the reveal of this villain’s origin was such a collective “CALLED IT” moment for the entire fanbase that it deserves a mention here, not to mention the way Dabi went about doing it. As one of the major villains of the series, Dabi is a formidable antagonist. His Blueflame Quirk made him a lethal force, and his singed appearance made him look all the more sinister. In fact, his control over fire could only be rivaled by that of the Pro Hero Endeavor. This makes sense, of course, because Dabi is Endeavor’s long-lost son. In a public broadcast, Dabi reveals himself to be Toya Todoroki, the eldest child of Endeavor and brother to hero-in-training Shoto. Endeavor had been attempting to pass his Quirk down to his offspring, hoping that they would inherit both his Fire and his wife’s Ice. However, the physical and emotional abuse that Endeavor inflicted onto his family drove Toya insane. Worse still, Toya’s own body couldn’t withstand the flames he created, putting him in danger whenever he ignited his body. As he grew older and fell into the League of Villains, he adopted the name “Dabi” and made it his life’s mission to reveal Endeavor’s shame and bring an end to his legacy. Attack on Titan The twist: Literally all of them (take your pick) One of the main draws for the global phenomenon that was Attack on Titan was its sprawling story. That narrative, of course, was rife with so many twists that they would probably need their own article to keep track of them all. There’s Eren emerging from a Titan after taking down a horde, followed shortly by Titans in the walls. There’s also Annie being the Female Titan as she works undercover. Not to be outdone, Reiner revealed that he and Bertholdt were the Armored and Colossal Titans responsible for the fall of Wall Maria. Historia is royalty, Titans were humans, Eren and co. live on an island where their species is sent for punishment while the rest of the world exists in a more modern society. Oh, and can’t forget about Eren’s own time loop kicking off the events of the series. Attack on Titan is practically infamous for all of its shocking turns, so we feel comfortable in saying that all of its twists deserve a place on this list. ERASED The twist: The killer's identity The only thing better than a good old-fashioned murder mystery is a murder mystery that takes place across time. In ERASED , 29-year-old Satoru Fujinuma inexplicably possesses an involuntary power called “Revival,” which allows him to go back in time to moments before a life-threatening event and prevent it. After his own mother is murdered for digging too deeply into a child murder case from Satoru’s childhood, Satoru’s revival sends him back a full 18 years, placing his present-day consciousness in his 11-year-old body. This gives him the opportunity to discover the killer’s identity in the past, prevent them from murdering children, and stop his mother’s death in the future, all in one fell swoop. Throughout the course of the series, Satoru manages to protect his mother and new friends from any future harm caused by the killer. It isn’t until after he assures their safety that the killer makes himself known. It’s revealed to be Gaku Yashiro, Satoru’s homeroom teacher and a once-trusted father figure of his. Even then, it takes years for Satoru to be able to reveal this information and get one last swing at his former companion when he returns to the future. SCHOOL-LIVE! The twist: Yuki's true school life The first episode of SCHOOL-LIVE! is deceptively cheery and unassuming. Yuki Takeya seemingly lives a peaceful life at Megurigaoka Private High School. As part of the “School Living Club” supervised by their teacher, Yuki, Kurumi Ebisuzawa, Yuuri Wakasa, and Miki Naoki all live at the school with their pet dog Taroumaru. During the day, Yuki attends class regularly. She has pleasant conversations with the other students and enjoys being at school. Unfortunately, none of it is real. By the end of the first episode, it’s revealed that Yuki’s rosy school life is all an ongoing delusion. In actuality, she lives in the midst of a zombie outbreak that has claimed much of the school population as well as the outside world. She and the other members of the School Living Club are the only immediate survivors. The final moments of the first episode, which reveals blood-stained classrooms and zombies lurking the schoolgrounds, are a stark contrast to the lighthearted nature of Yuki’s fantasy. Paranoia Agent The twist: The truth behind Lil’ Slugger The streets of Japan are stalked by a criminal that can only be described as a “serial batter.” In Paranoia Agent , Lil’ Slugger glides through alleyways on inline skates, assaulting seemingly random victims with his golden bat. His first victim was Tsukiko Sagi, a character designer struggling to come up with a new mascot after her previous creation, a pink dog named Maromi, became a massive success. The media kicks up a frenzy over Lil’ Slugger’s identity as more and more victims come forward, many of them seemingly grateful to the assailant for attacking them and taking them away from their other struggles. While it might have been easy to pin his identity down to one person or another, the truth is far more complex than that. Lil’ Slugger is, for all intents and purposes, merely an entity. An entity through which people can focus and scapegoat for all of their worldly troubles as opposed to facing them head-on. The idea was first instigated by Tsukiko herself, who came up with Lil’ Slugger years prior. As a child, she had a puppy named Maromi whom she loved. However, a menstrual cramp caused her to let go of Maromi’s leash, accidentally letting the dog run into oncoming traffic. Fearing punishment from her father, she invented “Lil’ Slugger” and pinned it on him. From that point onward, Lil’ Slugger became the embodiment of escapism, and he could only be stopped by Tsukiko confronting her own woes. The Promised Neverland The twist: Grace Field House 11-year-old Emma lives an idyllic life with her fellow orphans at the Grace Field House orphanage. They spend their days in safety and comfort under the watchful eye of their “Mom,” Isabella, who only asks them to never stray too far from the orphanage. All the while, they further their education and are raised to be intelligent young children. Among them, Emma and her two best friends, Norman and Ray, are particularly bright. And like any orphanage, kids hope to be adopted by new parents and sent off to a loving home. However, the truth behind adoption in The Promised Neverland is far more cruel than they could have expected. One night, an orphan named Conny is adopted and taken away. Emma and Norman attempt to follow Conny, hoping to return her favorite stuffed animal to her before she leaves. Instead, they find Conny’s dead body and the existence of monsters. In reality, the children of Grace Field House are raised to be food for the high-class monsters on the outside under the guise of being adopted. Keeping this knowledge a secret, Emma and Norman (and eventually Ray) work to devise a plan to help the children escape their cruel fates and find a new life in the outside world. Naruto Shippuden The twist: Itachi's true intentions One of the biggest conflicts of the entire Naruto series was that of Sasuke and his older brother, Itachi. In the past, Itachi was a prodigal ninja, joining the Anbu assassination team at age 11. By the time he was older, Itachi was an inspiration for Sasuke, and the two of them shared a strong brotherly bond. One night, however, Itachi slaughtered his entire clan and spared only Sasuke. He later joined the Akatsuki organization of powerful exiled ninja. This drove Sasuke to follow in his footsteps as a prodigy in his own right, all to avenge his family and kill Itachi. After years of training and betrayal, Sasuke gained enough power to take on Itachi in Naruto Shippuden . As their battle reaches an end and Itachi draws his final breaths, he finally manages to reveal the truth of their fateful night to his little brother. In his career, Itachi had learned that the Uchiha clan was planning a coup against the Leaf Village due to distrust between the two factions. Itachi was initially torn between loyalty to his clan and to the Village he swore to protect. But when Danzo Shimura threatened to eliminate Sasuke along with the rest of the clan, Itachi took on the deed himself in order to protect his beloved brother. He then joined Akatsuki while retaining his loyalty to the Leaf Village. Fruits Basket The twist: Akito's truth Each member of the cursed Soma family comes with their own formative trauma in Fruits Basket . Much of it was dispensed at the hands of the head of the family, Akito. If each major member of the family is aligned with a figure from the Chinese zodiac myth, then Akito is said to be the “god” who invited all the animals to the banquet. Despite Akito’s frailness, they are physically and emotionally abusive to the other Somas, all while touting their blood as an unbreakable connection that they could never hope to sever. Of course, Akito wouldn’t be a Soma without baggage of their own. Throughout the course of the series, it is revealed that despite masquerading as a man, Akito is actually biologically female. Her mother, Ren, was ashamed of having given birth to a head of the family who was a woman. Having grown jealous of Akito for receiving more attention from her husband, Ren raised Akito as male. This remained a closely-guarded secret among the family, and Akito’s struggle with keeping this secret caused her to lash out at the rest of the clan.

item.title: Fruits Basket -prelude- to Release in U.S., Canada Theaters on February 16, 2026
item.author: Kyle Cardine
item.description: Tickets are on sale now for the next Crunchyroll Anime Nights event
item.content:encoded: Just in time for Valentine's Day, the next Crunchyroll Anime Nights outing will feature a special screening of Fruits Basket -prelude- ! The 2022 feature-length episode will screen in the United States and Canada for one night only on February 16, 2026, in an English dub format. Tickets are now on sale at the links below. Fruits Basket -prelude- United States and Canada Release Date: February 16, 2026 Format: English Dub Synopsis: Celebrate love this Valentine's with Crunchyroll Anime Nights! Before there was Tohru and Kyo—there was Katsuya and Kyoko. Discover the turbulent beginning of Tohru’s mom and her dark past, and the man who breathed new hope into her. Watch the evolution of their love story and the birth of the Honda family, as this chapter completes the full adaptation of the heartwarming Fruits Basket story. The original Fruits Basket manga by Natsuki Takaya was serialized in Hana to Yume from 1998 to 2006 for a total of 23 volumes. Yoshihide Ibata directed Fruits Basket -prelude- at TMS Entertainment. Taku Kishimoto handle the screenplay, with Masaru Shindo on character designs and Masaru Yokoyama on music. Fruits Basket -prelude- followed the broadcast of the newest Fruits Basket anime series which aired from 2019 to 2021. Crunchyroll currently streams the 2019 Fruits Basket anime series and describes it as such: Tohru Honda thought her life was headed for misfortune when a family tragedy left her living in a tent. When her small home is discovered by the mysterious Soma clan, she suddenly finds herself living with Yuki, Kyo, and Shigure Soma. But she quickly learns their family has a bizarre secret of their own: when hugged by the opposite sex, they turn into the animals of the Zodiac!

item.title: Gintama: Yoshiwara in Flames Anime Film Shares Battle Trailer Featuring Kagura and Abuto
item.author: Kara Dennison
item.description: The film premieres February 13, 2026 in Japan
item.content:encoded: The heat is on in the latest trailer for Gintama: Yoshiwara in Flames ! The film, which premieres February 13, 2026 in Japan, has just dropped a new "battle trailer" featuring the epic fight between Kagura and Abuto. Check out a sample of how the battle will look on the big screen: Gintama: Yoshiwara in Flames Battle Trailer Key visual Naoya Ando ( Paradox Live THE ANIMATION ) directs New Gintama the Movie: Yoshiwara in Flames with Gintama series director Yoichi Fujita supervising at BN Pictures. The script is written by Taku Kishimoto ( Ranking of Kings , DON’T TOY WITH ME, MISS NAGATORO ) with character designs drawn by Shinji Takeuchi, who is returning from the rest of the franchise. Crunchyroll currently streams the original Gintama anime, describing the series as such: In a world where aliens have invaded Edo Period Japan, skyscrapers, trains and motor bikes have replaced the simple life of Earth inhabitants. One man however, still carries the soul of a samurai, Gintoki Sakata, otherwise known as Yorozuya Gin-san. As reckless as he is, Gintoki carries his own resolve and is ready to take on any challenge with his fellow companions. Source: Official YouTube Channel

item.title: Voice Actor Hiroshi Kamiya Shares New Music Video "Sunrise Orange"
item.author: Mikikazu Komatsu
item.description: 11th mini-album "Share Music" hits stores January 28
item.content:encoded: Voice actor Hiroshi Kamiya (Takashi Natsume in Natsume's Book of Friends , Levi in Attack on Titan ) released a music video for "Sunrise Orange," the lead track from his latest 11th mini-album "Share Music," on his music label Kiramune's official YouTube channel . Hiroshi Kamiya "Sunrise Orange" Music Video While working as a voice actor, Kamiya made his solo artist debut in 2009, releasing eight singles, two full albums, and 11 mini-albums to date. "Share Music" released in Japan on January 28 as his first mini-album in a year and a half since the release of his previous tenth mini-album "HARETTER" in July 2024. "Share Music" All-Song Preview "Share Music" deluxe edition CD jacket "Share Music" regular edition CD jacket Hiroshi Kamiya RELATED: Voice Actor Hiroshi Kamiya Releases New Music Video "Shujinkou ni Naru Monogatari" Source: Kiramune official YouTube channel

item.title: KenIchi: The Mightiest Disciple Sequel Manga Announced
item.author: Liam Dempsey
item.description: The original series concluded its run in 2014
item.content:encoded: More than a decade after the series concluded, author Shun Matsuena announced on his X (formerly Twitter) account today that KenIchi: The Mightiest Disciple will return on March 25 in the form of a sequel manga, subtitled "Tatsujin-hen" ("Masters Arc"), on Shogakukan's Sunday Webry platform. RELATED: Crunchyroll Manga: Full List of Every Series You Can Read Right Now The KenIchi: The Mightiest Disciple manga, which was a remake of Matsuena's Tatakae! Ryozanpaku Shijo Saikyo no Deshi that ran from October 1999 to February 2022, was serialized in Weekly Shonen Sunday magazine from April 2002 to September 2014. The series was collected into 61 volumes and had a reported circulation of 12 million copies in 2012. Hajime Kamegaki directed and TMS Entertainment produced the 50-episode TV anime adaptation that aired from October 2006 to September 2007, which was followed by the "The Attack of Darkness" 11-episode OVA series in 2012-2014, directed by Hiroshi Ishiodori at studio Brain's Base. Crunchyroll describes the anime: Kenichi, who has been bullied but wishes to be strong, joins Karate club upon entering senior high school. However, he gets picked on by the other pupils in the club everyday. One day, Kenichi meets Miu, a beautiful girl who has just transferred from another high school. On Miu's suggestion, Kenichi decides to be a disciple of Ryozanpaku, a mysterious training establishment. Kenichi could not imagine the brutal training he receives from the martial arts masters there... However, now Kenichi is marked by Ragnarok, a street gang group, and he is constantly defending himself from them. Source: Shun Matsuena on X (formerly Twitter)

item.title: Baki Manga Series Releases 35th Anniversary Illustration, Opening Movie
item.author: Mikikazu Komatsu
item.description: Official theme song "BAKI MENTAL" will be available on February 4
item.content:encoded: The official X (formerly Twitter) for Keisuke Itagaki's martial arts manga series Baki 's 35th anniversary project released a memorial illustration newly-drawn by Itagaki. 35th anniversary illustration Additionally, the Baki series' official YouTube channel started streaming the 35th project's opening movie featuring the memorable scenes from the story. The project's official theme song featured in the video, "BAKI MENTAL" was produced by Pocyomkin of Japanese hip-hop unit Gaki Ranger—who released the Baki-inspired track "Rap Grappler Gaki" in 2002—alongside singer RUDEBWOY FACE and rapper TAKE-M. Baki 35th Anniverrsary Project Opening Movie "BAKI MENTAL" will be available digitally on February 4. "BAKI MENTAL" jacket RELATED: Baki-Dou: The Invincible Samurai Anime Premiere Date Announced The manga series' first installment Baki the Grappler started serialization in Akita Shoten's Weekly Shonen Champion magazine in October 1991, then its sequels Baki (199-2005), Baki Hanma (2006-2012), Baki-Dou (2014-2018), Bakidou (2018-2023), and Baki Rahen (2023-ongoing). Under the title Baki-Dou: The Invincible Samurai , the anime adaptation of the fourth installment will start streaming on Netflix worldwide on February 26, 2026. Source: Baki 35th anniversary project official X (formerly Twitter) , Baki series official YouTube channel

item.title: Soala Releases Tune In to the Midnight Heart Anime Ending Song Music Video
item.author: Mikikazu Komatsu
item.description: The new rom-com series is now available on Crunchyroll
item.content:encoded: The official YouTube channel for singer-songwriter Soala started streaming a music video for her latest digital single song "Koe no Kiseki" ("The Traces of Voice"), which is now featured as the ending theme for the winter 2026 TV anime Tune In to the Midnight Heart . Soala "Koe no Kiseki" Music Video Tune In to the Midnight Heart Anime Creditless Ending Movie Soala is a singer-songwriter born in Aichi Prefecture and based in Osaka, whose songs have surpassed 200 million cumulative streams. She provided the following comment on "Koe no Kiseki," released digitally on January 28, on her official website . My new song "Koe no Kiseki" is a heart-wrenching track themed around "continuing to search for the voice of the one I loved." In creating this song, I focused on incorporating phrases sung with a voice that sounds like it could burst into tears at any moment, and consciously crafted a powerful melody and lyrics that evoke the image of someone desperately searching for the voice of their beloved. I hope this song can gently accompany your heart, blending with each listener's own memories and feelings. I hope you'll listen to this song while remembering the voices that saved you during difficult times in your life, or the voices of those dear to you. May it reach the hearts of many. "Koe no Kiseki" digital jacket Soala artist photo RELATED: Hoshimachi Suisei Shares Tune In to the Midnight Heart Anime Opening Song Music Video Tune In to the Midnight Heart Anime Trailer The TV anime adaptation of Masakuni igarashi's Tune In to the Midnight Heart romantic comedy manga premiered in Japan on January 6, 2026. Crunchyroll streams the anime for members in North America, Central America, South America, Europe, Africa, Oceania, the Middle East, CIS, Indian Subcontinent and Southeast Asia. Crunchyroll describes Tune In to the Midnight Heart as such: Meet Arisu Yamabuki, heir to a powerful conglomerate who seeks perfection in life. He listened to the radio program Midnight Heart Tune until the host, Apollo, vanished. Upon enrolling at Furin High and joining the broadcasting club, he meets four girls hoping to make a living off their voices. Arisu promises to make their vocal dreams come true and honor the promise he once made to Apollo. Source: Soala official YouTube channel / website

item.title: Moshi, Korekara Umarerunonara Anime Short Film Unveils Trailer, Visual
item.author: Mikikazu Komatsu
item.description: Miu Sakamoto will perform the theme song "Lullaby"
item.content:encoded: The official website for Toho's talent support project GEMSTONE Creative Label released a trailer and key visual for Moshi, Korekara Umarerunonara (translated as "If you were to be born from now on"), the only anime short in the project's forthcoming second omnibus film, GEMNIBUS vol.2 . Moshi, Korekara Umarerunonara Trailer Key visual Comment from Director Asuka Dokai: I sometime wonder what will the life yet to be born think about, and in what of this world will it find meaning to live? If you live, you will lose something and get hurt. Even so, I believe that the colors of the world I feel in fleeting moments can be shared with those who live alongside me in the present. I hope you will feel anew the warm touch of "being alive right now." With that sentiment in mind, I made this film. GEMSTONE Creative Label is a project launched by young employees at Toho with the aim of providing a space for creators to freely express their talent, regardless of format, media or prior achievements. The first omnibus film GEMNIBUS vol.1 released in June 2025, featuring the tokusatsu short Godzilla vs. Megalon and the sole anime short First Line (directed by China). GEMNIBUS vol.2 features six short films, and will screen for one week only from March 6 to 12 at the TOHO Cinemas Hibiya theater in Tokyo. The program's sole anime short Moshi, Korekara Umarerunonara was written and directed by Asuka Dokai, whose independently produced short films have been screened at film festivals both domestically and internationally. She is also known for her directorial works for the music video for YOASOBI 's 2023 song "Umi no Manimani" and the anime promotional video "Kimi ni Aeta!" for the Pokémon World Championships 2023. Additionally, Marihiko Hara provided the soundtrack music for the film, with singer Miu Sakamoto performing the theme song "Lullaby," featured in the trailer, with the lyrics written by herself. They recently also collaborated on the theme song "Luminance" (composed by Hara, lyrics by Sakamoto) for the kabuki-themed live-action film Kokuho . Source: "GEMSTONE Creative Label" official website / X (formerly Twitter)

item.title: Kill Blue Anime Casts Daisuke Sakuma as Assassin Shin Kohazame
item.author: Kara Dennison
item.description: The anime adaptation of Tadatoshi Fujimaki's manga premieres in April 2026
item.content:encoded: The upcoming anime adaptation of the Shonen Jump manga series Kill Blue has added another assassin to its roster. Idol Daisuke Sakuma (from Snow Man) will step into the role of Shin Kohazame, the pacifier-toting member of Yugang. This isn't Sakuma's first voice acting role. He has appeared as Makusa North in Black Clover , Sho Kireide in this year's remake of High School! Kimengumi and Hibiki Kaito in You and Idol Precure . As previously reported , Shunsuke Takeuchi will voice the protagonist Juzo Ogami in his adult form. Yuko Sanpei (Renton Thurston in Eureka Seven ) is cast as Ogami in his 12-year-old form. The other main voice cast members announced today include: Noren Mitsuoka voiced by Fuka Izumi (Sakura Yaotome in Dealing with Mikadono Sisters Is a Breeze ) Kotatsu Nekota voiced by Shuichiro Umeda (Naoto Watari in Watari-kun's ****** Is About to Collapse ) Tendo Tenma voiced by Takeo Otsuka (Teruaki Nakamura in OKITSURA: Fell in Love with an Okinawan Girl, but I Just Wish I Know What She's Saying ) Chisato Shiraishi voices by Atsumi Tanezaki (Anya Forger in SPY x FAMILY ) Eri Wanibuchi voiced by Yumi Uchiyama (Ema Sano in Tokyo Revengers ) The anime studio CUE will handle the animation production for the series. Tadatoshi Fujimaki ( Kuroko's Basketball ) serialized the Kill Blue manga in Shonen Jump from April 2023 to September 2025, relasing a total of 13 collected volumes. VIZ Media releases an English-language version of the series. VIZ Media describes story of Kill Blue as such: Juzo Ogami’s a legend. Even among hit men, his name sends shivers down spines. There’s never been a job he couldn’t handle—that is, until the day he wakes up as a teenager! But how has his body transformed? To find out, he’ll have to infiltrate the one place he thought he’d never find himself in again…school! Source : Kill Blue official X (formerly Twitter) account , Press Release

item.title: High School! Kimengumi Anime Casts Katsuyuki Konishi in Second Role
item.author: Joseph Luster
item.description: Konishi to voice Wakazo Temekono on top of role as Takuma Sessa
item.content:encoded: Voice actor Katsuyuki Konishi (Tengen Uzui in Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba ) already voices Takuma Sessa in the High School! Kimengumi anime, and now the series has him hopping in another role. Starting in episode 4, Konishi will voice school gang leader Wakazo Temekono, pictured above. The episode also marks the appearance of Sakugo Jidai, featuring the previously-announced voice work of Tomokazu Sugita (Gintoki Sakata in Gintama ). High School! Kimengumi Episode 4 Preview Kazuaki Seki (Perfume, Gen Hoshino music videos) directs High School! Kimengumi at Animation Studio Seven with series composition written by Shigeru Murakoshi ( ZOMBIE LAND SAGA ), character designs from Yuka Abe and in-show song production by Night Tempo. It is currently airing in Japan in the noitaminA block. High School! Kimengumi is the second installment in Shinzawa's Kimengumi (literally translated to "Funny Face Club") manga series. The first entry, Sannen Kimengumi ("Third Year Funny-face Club" ), was serialized in Weekly Shonen Jump from October 1980 to April 1982. When the main characters entered high school, the title changed to High School! Kimengumi and the sequel was serialized in the same magazine from April 1982 to July 1987. The story centers on the five members of Kimen-gumi — Rei Ichido, Go Reietsu, Kiyoshi Shusse, Jin Daima, and Dai Monboshi — all of whom attend Ichiou Junior High School. Each member possesses a wildly eccentric personality. Transfer student Yui Kawa and her classmate Chie Uru, intrigued by the group, begin spending time with them. Source: Comic Natalie

item.title: Voice Actor Kozo Shioya Passes Away at Age 71
item.author: Joseph Luster
item.description: Shioya voiced Majin Buu in the Dragon Ball series, and more
item.content:encoded: Image via Aoni Production Kozo Shioya, a veteran voice actor known for roles ranging from Abura Sumashi in the Gegege no Kitaro: Jigoku Hen anime to Majin Buu in Dragon Ball Z , has passed away. According to talent agency Aoni Production , Shioya passed away on January 20, 2026 at age 71 due to a cerebral hemorrhage. Other anime voice roles over the years included Genzo, Pappag and Edward Weevil in ONE PIECE , Motokichi Kinuta in World Trigger , Hammond Pleide in As a Reincarnated Aristocrat, I’ll Use My Appraisal Skill to Rise in the World and Dr. Sewashi in Mazinger Z: Infinity . Shioya returned to the Gegege no Kitaro series as Konaki Jijii, who he voiced in the 1996 series and a trio of feature films. Shioya was born in Kagoshima prefecture on August 18, 1955, and began his voice acting career in 1982. He is also the older brother of voice actor Yoku Shioya (Ryota Miyagi in Slam Dunk , Cosmo Yuki in Space Runaway Ideon ). According to Aoi Production, Kozo Shioya’s close family held a private funeral. Our thoughts go out to Shioya's colleagues and friends, and we wish to thank him for all of his contributions to the anime industry. Sources: Aoni Production ( 2 ), Anime News Network

item.title: Crunchyroll Anime Awards 2026: Your Ultimate Guide
item.author: Crunchyroll Official
item.description: Celebrate the 2026 Anime Awards with our constantly updating guide, with all the latest, including nominees, voting, categories, and more!
item.content:encoded: The 10th annual Crunchyroll Anime Awards are almost here ! Last year, fans worldwide voted for their favorite anime series, characters, creators, voice actors and more as part of a massive celebration of anime in Tokyo, Japan! With the awards headed back to Tokyo on May 23, 2026 at the Grand Prince Hotel Shin Takanawa, we're excited to rally once again to spotlight the world of anime! But you're probably wondering, what are the categories and nominees? How do you cast your vote? Where can I watch? Consider this your ultimate guide to the Crunchyroll Anime Awards 2026. We'll be updating this as we get new information from here until the winners are revealed, so make sure to bookmark this page and get ready! Crunchyroll Anime Awards 2026 Overview How It Works Categories Nominees How to Vote Hosts Where and How to Watch What are the Crunchyroll Anime Awards? Since 2017, this annual event recognizes and celebrates anime’s finest series, films, and the talented people who make them. Fans from around the world, along with a global panel of judges, cast their votes in a variety of categories, and the results are revealed at our live awards ceremony. More information can be found on the official Anime Awards website. How do the Crunchyroll Anime Awards work? The Crunchyroll Anime Awards follow two rounds of voting. In the first round, a panel of judges determines the six initial nominees for each of the show's categories. In the second round, the judges panel will vote alongside fans to decide the winner in each category, including Anime of the Year! All of the winners will be revealed live as part of the 2026 Crunchyroll Anime Awards on May 23, 2026. What are the Categories for the Crunchyroll Anime Awards 2026? Anime of the Year Film of the Year Best Continuing Series Best New Series Best Original Anime Best Animation Best Character Design Best Director Back Background Art Best Romance Best Comedy Best Action Best Isekai Anime Best Drama Best Slice of Life Best Main Character Best Supporting Character "Must Protect At All Costs" Character Best Anime Song Best Score Best Opening Sequence Best Ending Sequence Best Voice Artist Performance (Japanese, Arabic, Brazilian Portuguese, Castilian Spanish, English, French, German, Italian, Latin Spanish, Hindi) For more details on how categories are selected and how each category is defined, head over to the official Anime Awards website right here. Who are the Nominees for the Crunchyroll Anime Awards 2026? The nominees for the Crunchyroll Anime Awards 2026 have not yet been revealed! Stay tuned for updates. How do I Vote in the Anime Awards? At the moment, there is no official information yet. Stay tuned for updates! Who is Hosting the Crunchyroll Anime Awards 2026? The 2026 Anime Awards will once again be co-hosted by voice actor and performer Sally Amaki ( Tomo-chan Is a Girl! , Marvel Rivals ) and pop entertainer Jon Kabira, with the global pre-show hosted by Crunchyroll’s Lauren Moore and Tim Lyu. Crunchyroll Anime Awards 2026 revealed its preliminary music lineup, including Japanese singer Yoko Takahashi, who will take the stage to perform a 30th anniversary tribute to Neon Genesis Evangelion and its renowned theme “A Cruel Angel’s Thesis.” In addition, the live show will feature symphonic suites performed by the Tokyo Philharmonic Orchestra (with Conductor Hirofumi Kurita and Composer/Arranger Kazunori Miyake) that will accent the ceremony. Look forward to more performances to be announced soon! Where can I watch the Crunchyroll Anime Awards 2026? No official information yet. Make sure to bookmark this page and stay tuned for updates!

item.title: Crunchyroll Anime Awards 2026 Sets May 23 for 10th Anniversary, Yoko Takahashi to Perform
item.author: Nicholas Friedman
item.description: Find out more about the hosts, musical performances, categories, and when the nominees will be revealed!
item.content:encoded: Announced this morning, the Crunchyroll Anime Awards 2026 event is officially returning to Tokyo, Japan, for its 10th anniversary celebration on May 23, featuring a global pre-show, star-studded orange carpet, and iconic anime performances, all supporting the prestigious nominees across 32 award categories. Crunchyroll Anime Awards 2026 revealed its preliminary music lineup, including Japanese singer Yoko Takahashi, who will take the stage to perform a 30th anniversary tribute to Neon Genesis Evangelion and its renowned theme “A Cruel Angel’s Thesis.” Yoko Takahashi Neon Genesis Evangelion Key Visual. ©khara／Project Eva. In addition, the live show will feature symphonic suites performed by the Tokyo Philharmonic Orchestra (with Conductor Hirofumi Kurita and Composer/Arranger Kazunori Miyake) that will accent the ceremony. Look forward to more performances to be announced soon! Hirofumi Kurita Kazunori Miyake The Tokyo Philharmonic Orchestra The 2026 Anime Awards will once again be co-hosted by voice actor and performer Sally Amaki ( Tomo-chan Is a Girl!) and entertainer Jon Kabira, with the global pre-show hosted by Crunchyroll’s Lauren Moore and Tim Lyu. Jon Kabira Sally Amaki Lauren Moore Tim Lyu Crunchyroll also announced that nominees for the 2026 ceremony will be revealed on April 2 as global fan voting goes live. So get ready, because it’s almost time to fight for your favorite series, movies, creators, and performances across 32 categories. Nominees in each category will be selected from eligible anime titles that premiered between January 1 and December 31, 2025, via the independent 2026 Anime Awards judges panel. For more on categories, the latest on the event, and so much more, head over to Crunchyroll News’s Ultimate Guide to Crunchyroll Anime Awards 2026.

item.title: MY HERO ACADEMIA: All’s Justice Trailer Rolls Out Full Roster
item.author: Joseph Luster
item.description: Sequel launches on PS5, Xbox Series and Steam on February 6, 2026
item.content:encoded: The roster of all-star My Hero Academia brawler MY HERO ACADEMIA: All’s Justice is on full display in the latest trailer, which runs through all the playable characters from Kohei Horikoshi’s series. From Deku and All for One to the many heroes and villains in between—including new FINAL SEASON additions—you can see them in action in the video below. MY HERO ACADEMIA: All’s Justice Full Roster Trailer MY HERO ACADEMIA: All’s Justice hits PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X|S and PC (Steam) on February 6, 2026. Here’s how Bandai Namco describes it: Smash through My Hero Academia‘s final story arc and triumph over your foes in spectacular 3v3 battles! Follow Deku and the journeys of other characters in the Final War between Heroes and Villains, and experience the world-shaking, climactic clash between One For All and All For One in My Hero Academia: All’s Justice! My Hero Academia: All’s Justice is the super-powered 3D arena fighter where your favorite characters from the My Hero Academia franchise appear in their latest and most powerful forms, framed within a Refined Battle System that showcases their Quirks to their limits! You can stream all of the My Hero Academia anime, including My Hero Academia FINAL SEASON , right here on Crunchyroll! Source: Press release

item.title: Here Are the Top Fan Voted Anime Openings of 2025!
item.author: Jared Clemons
item.description: Did your favorite anime opening make the list?
item.content:encoded: 2025 is in the books and it was a fun year for anime. We had fan favorites make their return, new series crop up to become big hits, and a few finally have their swan song. Which is also shown here in the results of our overall 2025 anime op poll with the series represented. After a year of polls to determine each season’s best opening, I put all the top five’s back into one final poll to determine the best opening of 2025. Will the previous winners make a triumphant return or will a new series come out of nowhere to claim the top spot? #5: "INERTIA" by SawanoHiroyuki[nZk]:Rei Donghua: TO BE HERO X | 8% of votes | #2 pick of Spring 2025 | #4 pick of Summer 2025 The only series here to be featured in two consecutive seasons, it’s no surprise that TO BE HERO X manages to snag a spot in the overall top five. It’s certainly a powerful opening with how the animation entices you in, features a character that doesn’t show up for a while in the show, and of course, has a song that’ll become an ear worm for you pretty quickly. Given that it was around for half of 2025, it makes sense why it was able to wind up here as one of the top openings of the year. #4: "The Revo" by Porno Graffitti Anime: My Hero Academia | 9% of votes | #4 pick of Fall 2025 While we will get another chance to revisit the world My Hero Academia in 2026 (hello My Hero Academia: Vigilantes Season 2), the bulk of the series has come to an end. It’s so apt to not only bring back a past artist for the final opening, but the artist that did the very first opening of the series and to also reference that song in this final opening as well. That is the kind of reference you want for a final opening. Something that harkens back to the beginning, but also shows the evolution of the series, which is what they’re able to pull off here. #3: "Reawaker" by LiSA (feat. Felix of Stray Kids) Anime: Solo Leveling | 10% of votes | #1 pick of Winter 2025 Surprisingly, this is our only previous winner to make a return here. Given the ever growing popularity of Solo Leveling , it makes sense to see it here as it is a really good opening. The animation is tremendous and when you combine that with the dichotomy between LiSA and Felix’s vocals, it creates a gripping opening that will have you ready for every episode. This is definitely an opening you’ll want to add to your playlist, especially since it’s one of the best of the year. #2: "Kakumei Dochu" by Aina the End Anime: DAN DA DAN | 10% of votes | #3 pick of Summer 2025 Last year’s top opening of the year was the first DAN DA DAN opening, so how do you follow that up? The important thing is to not just do the same thing again, which the second opening of the series avoids. It’s a song that has much more buildup than its predecessor, but also has the funkyness that makes sense for DAN DA DAN . Combine that with an animation that showcases the bizarre nature of all of the characters and you’ve got an opening that not only fits in well with the series, but is able to complement the first. While the percentages don’t tell the full story, this did only manage to sneak into second place by just a single vote. #1: "Let's Just Crash" by Mori Calliope Anime: Gachiakuta | 12% of votes | #5 pick of Fall 2025 Our biggest jump from the seasonal polls to the final poll. Perhaps the few extra months that everyone had with this opening allowed more people to jump on board with it to give the extra push to our top spot. That and I’m sure fans of Mori Calliope helped as well. This is one heck of an opening. Mori’s vocals truly shine through in this as we’re able to see how much she can do with regard to her speed, melodic tones, and screaming that all come together to form this wild and fun song. It’s no easy task to make it to the number one spot for the entire year, so Gachiakuta and Mori should be proud to have reached the mountain top for 2025. Congratulations once again to Gachiakuta and Mori Calliope for being your top opening theme for 2025 and a big thank you to everyone who voted in these polls over the course of the year. Without you all, we obviously couldn’t do this so your input and votes are greatly appreciated. Let’s hope we continue to get a lot of banger openings throughout all of 2026!

item.title: Demon Slayer: Infinity Castle, Chainsaw Man: Reze Arc, More Anime Net 2026 Saturn Awards Nominations
item.author: Joseph Luster
item.description: Solo Leveling Season 2 makes an appearance in the TV noms
item.content:encoded: The nominees are in for the 53rd Saturn Awards, which aims to honor the best fantasy, sci-fi, horror, superhero and action-adventure movies, shows and releases of the past year. Anime received a handful of nods, including nominations for Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba Infinity Castle , Chainsaw Man - The Movie: Reze Arc , Attack on Titan the Movie: The Last Attack and The Colors Within in the Best International Animated Film category. The quartet of anime films find themselves up against China’s Ne Zha 2 and international co-production Stitch Head . In other categories, Solo Leveling Season 2 -Arise from the Shadow- earned a nomination for Best Animated Television Series or Event. It’s up against Creature Commandos , Harley Quinn , Marvel Zombies , Predator: Killer of Killers , Star Wars: Tales of the Underworld and Your Friendly Neighborhood Spider-Man . Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba Infinity Castle may have missed out on an Oscar nomination, but both it and Chainsaw Man - The Movie: Reze Arc are on board for further potential accolades after major wins at the box office. Both films were nominated for the Japan Academy Film Prize for Best Animation Film, the winner of which will be announced on March 13. The Chainsaw Man anime film was also named the highest rated overall film of 2025 on Letterboxd . Next up for Infinity Castle is a re-release in IMAX in Japan in IMAX ratio , marking the first-ever anime film to be released in the expanded 1.43:1 aspect ratio. The 53rd annual Saturn Awards will be presented live on Sunday, March 8, 2026 from the Hilton Los Angeles in Universal City, California. Source: Deadline

item.title: Sentenced to Be a Hero Anime Kicks off Mureed Fortress Arc with New Trailer and Visual
item.author: Kara Dennison
item.description: The dark fantasy anime is now streaming on Crunchyroll
item.content:encoded: This season's Sentenced to Be a Hero anime is off to its next port of call, kicking off the Mureed Fortress Arc in episode 4. As with the previous arc, a new trailer and "location visual" spotlight what's next for Xylo and his party. Check out the trailer: Sentenced to Be a Hero Episode 4 Trailer And the newest location visual: Sentenced to Be a Hero Episode 4 Location Visual Hiroyuki Takashima ( Mushoku Tensei: Jobless Reincarnation animation director) directs Sentenced to Be a Hero at Studio KAI, with series composition by Kenta Ihara ( Saga of Tanya the Evil ), character designs by Takeru Noda and music by Shunsuke Takizawa ( Kemono Michi: Rise Up ). Yoshitake Nakakoji serves as assistant director and creature designer, Nobutaka Ike serves as world designer and Kota Sugawa serves as prop designer. The anime is based on a series of novels by Rocket Shokai. Crunchyroll streams Sentenced to Be a Hero as it airs, describing the dark fantasy series thus: In a world where heroism is a punishment, Xylo Forbartz, a condemned goddess killer, battles endless hordes of monstrous abominations as part of Penal Hero Unit 9004. Death is no escape, only a cycle of resurrection and relentless combat. But when Xylo encounters a mysterious new goddess, their unlikely alliance sparks a rebellion that could shatter the chains of eternal punishment. Source: Official X (formerly Twitter) account

item.title: Yowayowa Sensei Anime Reveals More Cast in Teaser Trailer
item.author: Liam Dempsey
item.description: Hiroshi Ishiodori directs the comedy series at studio Brain's Base
item.content:encoded: A teaser trailer was released for the Yowayowa Sensei anime before its planned April 2026 broadcast today, giving us our first look at the school comedy series and introducing four additional cast members. Yowayowa Sensei Anime Teaser Trailer The newly announced cast members are as follows, joining Kakeru Hatano (Akihito) and Marika Kono (Hiyori): Mizuki Mukubayashi voiced by Yurie Igoma (Ruby in 【OSHI NO KO】 ) Yuki Yukishita voiced by Yu Sasahara (Puiniru in Puniru is a Kawaii Slime ) Kaya Kuguri voiced by Yuko Natsuyoshi (Kaguya in Cosmic Princess Kaguya! ) Akemi Abikura voiced by Mai Nakahara (Byakuya in The Magical Girl and the Evil Lieutenant Used to Be Archenemies ) Mizuki Mukubayashi Yuki Yukishita Kaya Kuguri Akemi Abikura RELATED: Yowayowa Sensei Anime Unveils Teaser Visual, Main Voice Cast, April 2026 Premiere Hiroshi Ishiodori ( An Archdemon's Dilemma: How to Love Your Elf Bride ) directs the Yowayowa Sensei anime at studio Brain's Base, with series composition by Yoshifumi Fukushima, character designs by Naoki Aisaka ( Gakuen Basara: Samurai High School chief animation director) and music by Akifumi Tada and Rina Tayama ( Secrets of the Silent Witch ). As previously reported , the series' synopsis reads (translated): Hiyori Hiwamura, a newly-assigned teacher, is feared by her students as "Kowakowa Sensei" (Scary Scary Teacher) who will curse them if she gets upset. But in reality, she is just "Yowayowa Sensei" (Weak Weak Teacher) with zero physical strength, a weak voice, and every single stat at the absolute lowest level! Abekura-kun, the student in charge of Hiwamura-sensei's class, accidentally learns her secret. Determined to help her clear up the students' misunderstanding so her clumsy efforts will pay off, he gets swept up in thrilling, heart-pounding trouble while supporting his beloved Hiwamura-sensei! Source: Comic Natalie

item.title: Nia Liston: The Merciless Maiden Anime Announced for October 2026
item.author: Liam Dempsey
item.description: A teaser trailer and visual were revealed, along with main cast and staff
item.content:encoded: An official website and X (formerly Twitter) account opened today revealed that a TV anime adaptation of Umikaze Minamino, JISHAKU and Katana Canata's Nia Liston: The Merciless Maiden light novel series is in production for a planned October 2026 broadcast. It's the second work of Minamino's to be adapted, following this season's Kunon the Sorcerer Can See . The announcement came with a teaser trailer and visual, along with cast and production staff details. As previewed in the trailer below, Honoka Inoue (Anne in Anne Shirley ) voices the titular Nia Liston. Nia Liston: The Merciless Maiden Anime Teaser Trailer Nia Liston: The Merciless Maiden Anime Teaser Visual RELATED: Nippon Sangoku Anime Announced for April 2026 Motoki Nakanishi ( Love Is Indivisible by Twins ) directs the Nia Liston: The Merciless Maiden anime at studio KONAMI animation, with series composition by Gigaemon Ichikawa ( The Fruit of Evolution ), character designs by Yuki Ito and Kana Tosaoka and music by Naoki Endo ( Love Live! Nijigasaki High School Idol Club ). Haruta Segi serves as assistant director and Hiromi Kikuta serves as sound director. J-Novel Club publishes an official English version of the original light novels and describes the story: A great hero lies undefeated on her deathbed, regretting that she never found a foe who could take her down. She never intended to meet her fate anywhere other than on the battlefield, and her peaceful demise has her wishing desperately that things could have been different... But when she suddenly comes to in the body of the young, frail Nia Liston, she’s got an all-new fight ahead of her. Nia is a sickly child, meaning she has a long way to go before she’s in fighting form. Now that she’s been given a second shot at life, however, she’s determined to get what she wants. She’s going to live—and die—for battle this time! Make way for the Merciless Maiden! Source: Comic Natalie

item.title: From Old Country Bumpkin to Master Swordsman Season 2 Anime Releases New Trailer, Teaser Visual
item.author: Liam Dempsey
item.description: The adventure continues in July 2026
item.content:encoded: A brand-new trailer and teaser visual were released for From Old Country Bumpkin to Master Swordsman Season 2 before its planned July 2026 broadcast today. The visual depicts Beryl in a bout with his father alongside a tagline that reads, "To go beyond the strongest—" From Old Country Bumpkin to Master Swordsman Season 2 Anime Trailer From Old Country Bumpkin to Master Swordsman Season 2 Anime Teaser Visual RELATED: From Old Country Bumpkin to Master Swordsman Season 2 Anime Broadcasts in July 2026 Akio Kazumi ( Loner Life in Another World ) continues to direct the From Old Country Bumpkin to Master Swordsman anime at studios Passione and Hayabusa Film, with series composition by Kunihiko Okada ( The Eminence in Shadow scriptwriter), character designs and chief animation direction by Satsuki Hayasaki ( Yatogame-chan Kansatsu Nikki ) and music by Yasuharu Takanashi ( Fairy Tail ). J-Novel Club publishes an official English version of the original light novels and describes the story: Beryl Gardinant, a self-proclaimed “humble old man,” is a sword instructor at his dojo in a rural, backwater village. In his younger years, he dreamed of glory as a master swordsman, but those days are long behind him. Out of the blue, he receives a visit from a famous former pupil who brings him world-shattering news—he’s been appointed as a special instructor for the knights of the Liberion Order! With his life now turned upside down, Beryl travels to the capital and reunites with some of his former students: elite knights, an ace wizard, and even an adventurer who’s attained the highest guild rank possible. But why do they all want his tutelage?! As far as he’s concerned, they clearly don’t need him anymore. Can Beryl live up to his new position? And will he ever get a moment’s peace away from his adoring students?! Source: Comic Natalie

item.title: SawanoHiroyuki[nZk] Releases Fate/strange Fake Anime Opening Song Music Video
item.author: Mikikazu Komatsu
item.description: Crunchyroll streams the A-1 Pictures-animated series
item.content:encoded: SawanoHiroyuki[nZk] , the vocal-focused project by composer Hiroyuki Sawano , released a music video for "PROVANT" on its official YouTube channel . The song is now featured as the opening theme for the winter 2026 TV anime Fate/strange Fake . The music video features Sawano himself, alongside the two guest vocalists of the song: Jean-Ken Johnny from MAN WITH A MISSION and TAKUMA from 10-FEET. SawanoHiroyuki[nZk]:Jean-Ken Johnny & TAKUMA "PROVANT" Music Video Fate/strange Fake Anime Creditless Opening Movie Sawano launched the "SawanoHiroyuki[nZk]" project in 2014, collaborating with various vocalists to provide vocal songs for numerous popular anime series, including MOBILE SUIT GUNDAM UNICORN , ALDNOAH.ZERO and Kabaneri of the Iron Fortress . "PROVANT" released digitally first on January 4, and ranked #29 in Oricon's weekly digital single charts in its second week. The CD single is set to release in Japan on February 18, 2026. "PROVANT" time-limited production edition CD jacket "PROVANT" regular edition CD jakcet Jean-Ken Johnny (MAN WITH A MISSION) TAKUMA (10-FEET) Hiroyuki Sawano Fate/strange Fake Anime Trailer Following the release of the prologue chapter, Fate/strange Fake -Whispers of Dawn- , in July 2023, the TV anime Fate/strange Fake premiered in Japan on January 3, 2026. Crunchyroll streams the anime as it airs for members in North America, Central America, South America, Europe, Africa, Oceania, the Middle East, CIS and Indian subcontinent. Crunchyroll describes Fate/strange Fake as such: In a Holy Grail War, Mages (Masters) and their Heroic Spirits (Servants) fight for the control of the Holy Grail—an omnipotent wish-granting device said to fulfill any desire. Years have passed since the end of the Fifth Holy Grail War in Japan. Now, signs portend the emergence of a new Holy Grail in the western American city of Snowfield. Sure enough, Masters and Servants begin to gather... A missing Servant class... Impossible Servant summonings... A nation shrouded in secrecy... And a city created as a battleground. In the face of such irregularities, the Holy Grail War is twisted and driven into the depth of madness. Let the curtain rise on a masquerade of humans and heroes, made to dance upon the stage of a false Holy Grail. Source: Hiroyuki Sawano official YouTube channel

item.title: Voice Actor Yurika Kubo Shares New Music Video "Poppin Bambi"
item.author: Mikikazu Komatsu
item.description: Kubo's second album "Shikanoko" releases in Japan today
item.content:encoded: The official YouTube channel for voice actor Yurika Kubo (Hanayo Koizumi in Love Live! , Kikyo Kushida in Classroom of the Elite ) started streaming a music video for "Poppin Bambi," the lead track from her latest second full-length album "Shikanoko." Yurika Kubo "Poppin Bambi" Music Video While performing as a member of Love Live! franchise' voice actor unit μ's, Kubo made her solo artist debut in February 2016, releasing five singles, two full albums and one min-album to date. 2026 marks the 10th anniversary of her solo artist career. Following the milestone, she decided to end her activities as a solo artist. "Shikanoko" is both her second and last album, which released in Japan on January 28. The album contains a total of 10 tracks, including four new songs, her fourth single song "Kawaikutte Ijiwarushichau" ( The Legendary Hero Is Dead! ending theme), and cover songs personally selected by Kubo herself. "Shikanoko" Album New Song Preview Video "Shikanoko" first press limited edition CD jacket "Shikanoko" regular edition CD jacket Yurika Kubo latest artist photo Source: Yurika Kubo official YouTube channel / website
